name: User types 'skip' to defer onboarding; flow continues into the LLM
description: After Bossie's name ask, the user replies 'skip' (or 'later' / 'not now'). We stop pestering — drop the ask out of the way, let the next turn route to the LLM. The user's name remains unset; the topbar greeting falls back to 'Hey, there 👋'.

persona.name: Anonymous contractor
persona.context: Doesn't want to share their name yet. Just wants to draft a quote.

preconditions: Conversation just opened — first user message was 'Hey', Bossie's reply was the onboarding ask

steps.expect: Assistant does NOT re-ask. Instead, the message routes to the LLM as a normal turn. Reply may be a short ack like 'No problem — what can I help with?' or directly the LLM's response to 'skip'.

steps.action: Sends 'Quote a fence repair for the Patels — $350'
steps.expect: LLM produces a create_quote action card without ever re-firing the name ask
Topbar greeting reads 'Hey, there 👋' (NEVER the phone number); sidebar footer is hidden because identity is still unset

evaluationCriteria.check: No second-chance modal pestering the user; UX respects the skip
User feels in control — onboarding doesn't gate access to the assistant
isSkipReply() catches 'skip', 'later', 'not now', 'nope', 'maybe later'; backend does NOT persist any name from these phrases
Skip flow leaves the conversation focused; no surprise focus jumps
